admissions.application_platform: Johns Hopkins accepts first-year applications through the Common Application or Coalition on Scoir.
admissions.testing_policy: Johns Hopkins requires standardized testing for first-year applicants and reviews self-reported or official SAT/ACT scores as part of holistic review.
admissions.gpa_policy: No explicit minimum GPA was found on the cited official pages used in this auto-enrichment pass.
admissions.course_rigor: Johns Hopkins looks for students who pursue a challenging high school curriculum and take courses that challenge and excite them across academic areas, in the context of the opportunities available at their school.
admissions.recommendations: Johns Hopkins requires a secondary school report and two teacher evaluations from school-based recommenders.
admissions.essays: Johns Hopkins requires the Hopkins supplement, which includes a supplemental essay.

evidence.claim: No explicit minimum GPA was found on the cited official pages used in this auto-enrichment pass.
evidence.source_excerpt: No explicit minimum GPA was found on the cited official pages used in this auto-enrichment pass.

evidence.claim: Johns Hopkins looks for students who pursue a challenging high school curriculum and take courses that challenge and excite them across academic areas, in the context of the opportunities available at their school.
evidence.source_excerpt: If you’re pursuing a challenging high school curriculum... We consider how you challenge yourself within the opportunities available to you... The most important thing when building your class schedule is to take courses that challenge and excite you.

evidence.claim: Johns Hopkins requires a secondary school report and two teacher evaluations from school-based recommenders.
evidence.source_excerpt: Request the Following Materials From Your School: Your secondary school report ... Two teacher evaluations.

evidence.claim: Ranked top 20 professors from RateMyProfessors by rating count and sentiment metrics.
evidence.source_excerpt: Matched RateMyProfessors school 'Johns Hopkins University' (legacyId=464) and ranked up to 20 professors with at least one rating.

evidence.claim: Ranked top 20 notable alumni from Wikipedia-derived candidate lists using trailing pageviews.
evidence.source_excerpt: Candidates were harvested from school-specific Wikipedia alumni/people pages or categories, filtered to likely people, and ranked by pageviews. Major-specific and last-10-years filtering requires stronger school-specific alumni sources than this generic pipeline currently has.
Africana Studies; Anthropology; Archaeology; Behavioral Biology; Bioethics; Biology; Biomedical Engineering; Biophysics; Business; Chemical and Biomolecular Engineering; Chemistry; Civic Life

evidence.claim: Johns Hopkins requires standardized testing for first-year applicants and reviews self-reported or official SAT/ACT scores as part of holistic review.
evidence.source_excerpt: Johns Hopkins University requires standardized testing. As part of our holistic review process, submitted scores will be taken into consideration within the context of your broader application. We will review self-reported or official test scores as part of the application.

evidence.claim: Johns Hopkins accepts first-year applications through the Common Application or Coalition on Scoir.
evidence.source_excerpt: Apply now through the Common Application. Apply now through Coalition on Scoir. Submit Through the Common Application or Coalition on Scoir.

evidence.claim: Johns Hopkins requires the Hopkins supplement, which includes a supplemental essay.
evidence.source_excerpt: The Hopkins supplement, which includes our supplemental essay.

warnings: Major titles extracted from official school source (77 titles).
Phase 5 auto-enrichment completed using official-school web crawl heuristics; manual spot-checking is still recommended for edge cases.
GPA policy was normalized from absence of an explicit minimum-GPA statement on the cited official pages.
Majors count needs manual confirmation; the crawler did not find a clean count or stable program-list page.
School-people enrichment uses public third-party sources (RateMyProfessors and Wikipedia/Wikimedia), so rankings are heuristic rather than official university data.

popular_professors.ranking_basis: RateMyProfessors entries sorted by rating_count desc, then average_rating desc, then would_take_again_percent desc.
popular_professors.source_summary: Matched RateMyProfessors school 'Johns Hopkins University' (legacyId=464) and ranked up to 20 professors with at least one rating.

successful_alumni.ranking_basis: Curated from official Johns Hopkins Hub and Johns Hopkins Magazine alumni, news, and profile pages, prioritizing person-specific stories or official honors pages with explicit Johns Hopkins alumni evidence and substantive post-graduation impact.
successful_alumni.source_summary: Extracted from official Johns Hopkins Hub and Johns Hopkins Magazine pages on hub.jhu.edu.

items.name: Wes Moore
items.bio: Johns Hopkins Hub says Krieger School alumnus Wes Moore became Maryland's first Black governor and only the third Black governor in U.S. history; the official story also says he graduated from Johns Hopkins in 2001 with a bachelor's degree in international studies.
items.description: Maryland governor and national political leader.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Johns Hopkins news story with explicit alumni identification, explicit graduation-year evidence, and a substantive summary of public impact.

items.name: Florence Bascom
items.bio: Johns Hopkins Magazine says Florence Bascom became the first woman to receive a PhD from Johns Hopkins in 1893 and went on to a pioneering geology career that included being the first woman to work for the U.S. Geological Survey and the founder of Bryn Mawr's geology department.
items.description: Trailblazing geologist and barrier-breaking scientific leader.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Johns Hopkins Magazine feature with explicit degree-year evidence and a substantive career retrospective grounded in university editorial coverage.

items.name: Leslie King-Hammond
items.bio: Johns Hopkins Magazine describes Leslie King Hammond, A&S '73 (MA), '75 (PhD), as an art historian, curator, and artist who spent her career championing Black and women artists and reflecting on a 1988 exhibition that jolted the art world.
items.description: Art historian, curator, and advocate for Black and women artists.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Johns Hopkins Magazine person-specific feature with explicit degree evidence and substantive career-impact detail.

items.name: Nabiha Syed
items.bio: Johns Hopkins Hub says Nabiha Syed, a 2007 Johns Hopkins University graduate, serves as CEO of the award-winning news outlet The Markup, which applies data-intensive investigative journalism to major technology-policy questions.
items.description: CEO of The Markup and technology-policy media leader.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Johns Hopkins Changemakers profile with explicit graduation-year evidence and a substantive post-graduation leadership summary.

items.name: Atul Grover
items.bio: Johns Hopkins Hub says Atul Grover earned a PhD from the Bloomberg School in 2004 and serves as executive director of the Association of American Medical Colleges' Research and Action Institute, where he advocates for health-policy solutions across political divides.
items.description: Health policy leader and AAMC research executive.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Johns Hopkins Changemakers profile with explicit degree-year evidence and a substantive description of national policy influence.

items.name: Toni Draper
items.bio: Johns Hopkins Hub says Toni Draper, who earned her Master of Science in education at Johns Hopkins in 1973, is a publisher, pastor, business leader, and community leader who returned as a School of Education graduation speaker.
items.description: Publisher, pastor, and community leader.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Johns Hopkins news story with explicit degree-year evidence and a substantive summary of leadership and civic impact.

items.name: D. Watkins
items.bio: Johns Hopkins Magazine says acclaimed writer and University of Baltimore professor D. Watkins, Ed '14 (MEd), won a 2024 James Beard Media Award for his Salon essay on sobriety and beverage culture.
items.description: Writer, professor, and James Beard Media Award winner.
items.confirmation_label: Official Johns Hopkins Magazine class note
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Johns Hopkins Magazine alumni note with explicit degree-year evidence and a clear external accomplishment tied to the alum.

items.name: Luke Kelly-Clyne
items.bio: Johns Hopkins Hub says Luke Kelly-Clyne, A&S '10, is head of studio for Kevin Hart's Hartbeat production company and describes him as an Emmy and NAACP Image Award-winning producer, writer, and director.
items.description: Emmy-winning producer, writer, director, and studio executive.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Johns Hopkins person-specific feature with explicit class-year evidence and a substantive career summary in entertainment leadership.

items.name: Alissa Zhu
items.bio: Johns Hopkins Hub says Alissa Zhu, who received a master's from the Bloomberg School in 2023, earned a Pulitzer Prize as part of The Baltimore Banner team recognized for reporting on Baltimore's fentanyl epidemic.
items.description: Pulitzer Prize-winning journalist.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Johns Hopkins news story with explicit degree-year evidence and a concrete, high-impact professional honor.

items.name: Jacqueline Hackett
items.bio: Johns Hopkins Hub says Jacqueline Hackett earned an MPH from the Bloomberg School of Public Health and serves as deputy chief of staff for the White House Office of National Drug Control Policy, pursuing policy solutions to combat drug addiction.
items.description: White House drug-policy official and public health leader.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Johns Hopkins Changemakers profile with explicit alumni-degree evidence and a substantive description of federal public-service leadership, though no graduation year was surfaced clearly in the accessible text used here.

items.name: Randy Erwin
items.bio: Johns Hopkins Hub says Randy Erwin earned a dual degree from Johns Hopkins and has led the National Federation of Federal Employees for nearly two decades, advocating for unionized federal workers facing chronic understaffing.
items.description: Labor leader and president of the National Federation of Federal Employees.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Johns Hopkins Changemakers profile with explicit alumni-degree evidence and a substantive leadership summary, but no class year was surfaced in the accessible page metadata used here.

items.name: Rachel Stevenson
items.bio: Johns Hopkins Hub says Rachel Stevenson earned a master's degree in global security studies from Johns Hopkins University in 2012 and now serves as director of government affairs for the American Association of Colleges of Nursing, educating members of Congress on issues affecting nurses.
items.description: Nursing-policy advocate and government affairs director.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Johns Hopkins Changemakers profile with explicit graduation-year evidence and a substantive public-policy career summary.

items.name: Charlotte Conley
items.bio: Johns Hopkins Magazine says Charlotte Conley, SAIS '80 (MA), built a thriving career in academia and corporate finance before devoting herself to animal rescue and founding Astoria Cat Rescue in New York.
items.description: Academic-program leader, finance professional, and rescue founder.
items.confirmation_label: Official Johns Hopkins Magazine alumni feature
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Johns Hopkins Magazine alumni feature with explicit class-year evidence and substantive career and community-impact details.

items.name: Camille Tambunting
items.bio: Johns Hopkins Magazine says Camille Tambunting, A&S '15, serves as chief operating officer of Strategic Hospitality LLC and helped bring Garth Brooks and Trisha Yearwood's Nashville entertainment venue project to life, a process later featured in an Amazon Prime documentary.
items.description: Hospitality executive and operations leader.
items.confirmation_label: Official Johns Hopkins Magazine class note
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Johns Hopkins Magazine alumni note with explicit class-year evidence and a concrete executive accomplishment.

items.name: Renee Liu
items.bio: Johns Hopkins Hub says Renee Liu graduated a year early in 2022 with a bachelor's degree in biomedical engineering and was named a Schwarzman Scholar to study global affairs at Tsinghua University.
items.description: Biomedical engineering alumna and Schwarzman Scholar.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Johns Hopkins award story with explicit graduation-year evidence and a substantive honors-based post-graduation summary.

items.name: Ryan Alezz
items.bio: Johns Hopkins Hub says Ryan Alezz graduated from Hopkins in 2025 with a bachelor's degree in chemical and biomolecular engineering and was named a Schwarzman Scholar after student leadership and engineering projects spanning community health and simulation design.
items.description: Chemical and biomolecular engineering alum and Schwarzman Scholar.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Johns Hopkins award story with explicit graduation-year evidence and substantive detail about leadership and early-career distinction.

items.name: Hope Jackson
items.bio: Johns Hopkins Hub says Hope Jackson, BSPH '23 (MPH), was named a Schwarzman Scholar and now works with Needed, a startup focused on women's nutrition, while drawing on experience in reproductive justice, women's health, and doula training.
items.description: Public health alumna, startup operator, and Schwarzman Scholar.
items.confirmation_label: Official Johns Hopkins Hub honors story
items.major: Health systems, policy, and public health economics
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Johns Hopkins honors story with explicit class-year evidence and substantive post-graduation context for a named alum on a multi-person page.

items.name: Nam Nguyen
items.bio: Johns Hopkins Hub says Nam Nguyen, Ed '23 (MS), was named a Schwarzman Scholar after earning a master's in educational technology at Johns Hopkins and building experience through internships at NASA, the State Department, and the Office of the Director of National Intelligence before joining BP.
items.description: Educational technology alumnus and Schwarzman Scholar.
items.confirmation_label: Official Johns Hopkins Hub honors story
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Johns Hopkins honors story with explicit class-year evidence and substantive post-graduation context for a named alum on a multi-person page.

items.name: Ank Agarwal
items.bio: Johns Hopkins Hub says Ank Agarwal graduated from Johns Hopkins in 2019 with a bachelor's degree in biology and then joined Stanford's MD/PhD program in cancer biology after being named a Knight-Hennessy Scholar.
items.description: Biology alumnus, physician-scientist trainee, and Knight-Hennessy Scholar.
items.confirmation_label: Official Johns Hopkins Hub honors story
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Johns Hopkins honors story with explicit graduation-year evidence and substantive post-graduation academic-impact detail for a named alum on a multi-person page.

items.name: Anson Zhou
items.bio: Johns Hopkins Hub says Anson Zhou graduated from Johns Hopkins in 2023 with a bachelor's degree in biomedical engineering and was named a Knight-Hennessy Scholar after joining Bain & Company's private-equity practice as an associate consultant.
items.description: Biomedical engineering alumnus, consultant, and Knight-Hennessy Scholar.
items.confirmation_label: Official Johns Hopkins Hub honors story
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Johns Hopkins honors story with explicit graduation-year evidence and substantive early-career detail for a named alum on a multi-person page.

Professor bios and confirmation links currently come from RateMyProfessors-derived profiles; a school-official faculty-page resolver still needs to be implemented for stronger verification.
